title: Training Matryoshka Mixture-of-Experts for Elastic Inference-Time Expert Utilization
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of elastic inference in large-scale Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models. The authors identify a fundamental brittleness in current MoE models, where reducing the number of activated experts leads to sharp performance degradation. They propose Matryoshka MoE (M-MoE), a training strategy that instills coarse-to-fine granularity within the MoE's expert routing mechanism to enable true inference-time elasticity.
main_contributions: Identification of brittleness in current MoE models for elastic inference
Proposal of Matryoshka MoE (M-MoE) as a solution for elastic inference
Demonstration that current MoE models cannot deliver on the promise of true inference-time elasticity
Introduction of a layer-wise approach for expert routing in M-MoE

methodology.key_innovation: Layer-wise approach for expert routing with coarse-to-fine granularity
methodology.alternatives_explored: Randomizing expert count for global batch

key_results.expert_scaling: Increasing experts yields minimal performance gains while reducing experts causes sharp degradation
key_results.visualization: Figure 1 shows performance across varying numbers of activated experts

applications: Dynamic adaptation of computational footprint for diverse user requirements
